title: How can a task be spread out over several days?
excerpt: For example, if a task takes 12hrs and is spread out over 3 days, I’d like to see the task break down to 4hr increments so that my employees can take on multiple projects rather than cramming the 12hr project into one da…

posts.author: dagam (Dan Agam)
posts.cookedHtml: For example, if a task takes 12hrs and is spread out over 3 days, I’d like to see the task break down to 4hr increments so that my employees can take on multiple projects rather than cramming the 12hr project into one day. Even with a timeline for the task that states 3 days, I’m still seeing the event only on the Due Date in Workload view. Am I missing something?

posts.author: CharlotteK (Charlotte Kirkham)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Dan!

It sounds like you might want to have three total date columns (Day 1, Day 2, Day 3) in order to utilize the Workload View the way you’re describing.

Alternatively, you could use the “Effort” settings and use a Time Tracking column to determine the amount of time your team is using on the project. This way you can break up the tasks by sessions in the Time Tracking column or manually enter 4 hours per day/item.

If neither of these suggestions would be a good fit, let me know!
